description_long: This is a traditional water well with a circular stone base and a pitched roof made of reddish-orange tiles supported by wooden beams. The well features a wooden crank mechanism with a horizontal handle and a central spindle, likely used for raising and lowering a bucket. The stone base is polygonal and robust, providing stability and protection around the well opening. The roof is supported by two main vertical wooden posts and additional curved braces, offering shelter from rain and debris. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for outdoor urban or park environments. The design is simple and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements.
description: A classic stone water well with a tiled pitched roof and wooden crank mechanism, supported by sturdy wooden beams. The structure is practical and robust, with a polygonal stone base and no visible decorations.
description_view_0: Front view showing the tiled roof, wooden crank, and the open well mouth beneath the shelter.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden support beams, crank handle, and the depth of the roof structure.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the crank mechanism and stone base visible under the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden supports and crank handle from a different angle.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden crank and spindle mechanism is present at the center, used for drawing water.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A circular stone wall surrounds the well opening, approximately waist-high, with a wooden roof supported by posts.
